description: While most runner beans are tall climbers, 'Hestia' is a fantastic dwarf variety that grows into a bushy plant about 45cm tall. This makes it absolutely perfect for containers and window boxes on a balcony. It produces beautiful red and white bi-coloured flowers and heavy crops of stringless pods.

characteristics.waterNeeds: Very high — containers must stay moist

october: 🧹 Clear away plants after first frost

care.sowIndoors: Sow 5cm deep in deep pots or recycled toilet roll tubes. They have long roots and dislike being moved. Keep at 18–21°C.
care.sowOutdoors: After last frost (late May), sow directly into final pots 5cm deep. Space 20cm apart.
care.hardenOff: Move indoor plants outside during the day and bring back in at night for 7–10 days before final planting.
care.plantOut: Plant out once risk of frost has passed. If growing climbing varieties, ensure supports (wigwam) are in place first.
care.watering: Thirsty plants! Water daily in summer. Dry soil causes 'flower drop' where pods fail to form.
care.feeding: Feed with a high-potash liquid fertiliser once flowers appear to encourage more pods.
care.harvest: Pick pods when 15–20cm long. Regular picking every 2–3 days is essential to keep the plant producing.
care.overwintering: Annual — they will die at the first frost. Compost the plant but leave the roots in the soil if possible (they add nitrogen).

propagation.seedDescription: Large, beautiful kidney-shaped seeds, often purple with black speckles.

deadheading.notes: Not needed, but regular harvesting of pods acts in the same way to keep the plant productive.

seedSaving.method: Leave a few healthy pods on the plant at the end of the season until they turn brown and the seeds rattle inside. Dry thoroughly indoors.
seedSaving.seedPodDescription: Long, green, slightly rough-textured pods containing large, speckled seeds.

commonProblems: Flower Drop — caused by dry soil or lack of bees. Water well and plant flowers to attract pollinators.
Blackfly — aphids love new growth. Spray with water or soapy mix.
Slugs — protect young seedlings with copper tape or grit.
funFact: Runner beans are actually perennials in their native Central America, where they grow from tuberous roots. In the UK, we grow them as annuals because they can't survive our winters.
